Instructions for configuring Flask to trust proxy headers using Werkzeug's ProxyFix middleware.

# Purpose
When deploying a Flask application behind a reverse proxy or on certain Python hosting platforms, it is important to configure the application to correctly interpret the original client request information. Proxies typically forward requests to the local WSGI server, altering the perceived source of the request. To address this, HTTP servers use `X-Forwarded-` headers to convey the original request details. The Flask application can be configured to trust these headers by using the `ProxyFix` middleware from Werkzeug. This middleware adjusts the request object to reflect the original client data, but it must be configured with the correct number of proxies in the chain to ensure security and accuracy. Misconfiguration can lead to security vulnerabilities, so it is crucial to specify the exact number of proxies that set each header.
